_note: Curated by hand. Every URL and subPath verified via the GitHub API at curation time. To add an entry, edit this file and open a PR — see README.md for the quality bar.

entries.name: Superpowers
entries.description: Agentic skills framework: brainstorming, planning, writing-plans, executing-plans, debugging, code review, skill creation, and more.
entries.whyRecommended: The plugin most Claude Code power users install first. Covers the whole think → plan → build → ship arc.

entries.name: claude-mem
entries.description: Auto-captures what Claude does each session, compresses with the Agent SDK, and re-injects relevant context into future sessions.
entries.whyRecommended: Solves the cold-start problem on long-running projects. Lightweight enough to leave on always.

entries.name: gstack
entries.description: Garry Tan's full Claude Code setup — 23 opinionated tools acting as CEO, Designer, Eng Manager, Release Manager, Doc Engineer, and QA.
entries.whyRecommended: A complete PM/eng workflow toolkit. Use one or two skills, or the whole stack — gives you a team.

entries.name: Supermemory
entries.description: Lets Claude Code learn in real time, update its knowledge, and grow with you, backed by Supermemory's hosted vector store.
entries.whyRecommended: Cross-session memory with a real backend — alternative to claude-mem when you want hosted persistence and search.

entries.name: Skill Creator
entries.description: Anthropic's official skill for creating, editing, and validating new Claude Code skills, including evals.
entries.whyRecommended: Skip when you want to write a skill from scratch — this is the fastest path with a working scaffold and feedback loop.

entries.name: MCP Builder
entries.description: Build high-quality MCP servers with guided design patterns, well-shaped tools, and end-to-end testing.
entries.whyRecommended: The right starting point if you're building an MCP. Encodes the patterns that ship-quality servers actually use.

entries.name: PDF
entries.description: Read, write, edit, merge, split, and fill PDF forms — Anthropic's canonical PDF skill.
entries.whyRecommended: PDF tasks slow Claude down without a dedicated skill. This one's polished and well-tested.

entries.name: DOCX
entries.description: Create, read, and edit Word documents — track changes, comments, formatting, the lot.
entries.whyRecommended: Word doc workflows that previously took 10 prompts compress to one. Required if you ship to non-engineers.

entries.name: PPTX
entries.description: Build slide decks, pitch decks, and presentations programmatically — also reads + edits existing .pptx files.
entries.whyRecommended: PowerPoint output is consistent and reusable in a way ad-hoc generation isn't.

entries.name: XLSX
entries.description: Open, read, edit, and create .xlsx / .xlsm / .csv / .tsv files with proper formatting and formulas.
entries.whyRecommended: When the input or output is a spreadsheet, this skill replaces hand-rolled CSV parsing every time.

entries.name: Brand Guidelines
entries.description: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to artifacts that benefit from a polished look.
entries.whyRecommended: Saves the back-and-forth on visual decisions when you want output that looks intentional, not generic.

entries.name: Canvas Design
entries.description: Create visual art in PNG and PDF — posters, prints, design pieces — using a real design philosophy, not template slop.
entries.whyRecommended: When you want a static visual that doesn't look AI-generated. Pairs well with brand-guidelines.

entries.name: Doc Co-authoring
entries.description: Structured workflow for co-authoring proposals, technical specs, decision docs, and similar long-form content.
entries.whyRecommended: Long docs benefit from structure that ad-hoc generation skips. This skill keeps drafts coherent end-to-end.

entries.name: Internal Comms
entries.description: Resources for writing internal communications in the formats your company already uses.
entries.whyRecommended: Stop reinventing the standup / launch / postmortem template every time. Useful even if you adapt the formats.

entries.name: Web App Testing
entries.description: Drive a real browser, take screenshots, click through flows, and verify behavior — Anthropic's QA-flavored skill.
entries.whyRecommended: Catches the kind of bugs unit tests can't see. Pairs naturally with /ship-style workflows.

entries.name: Claude API
entries.description: Build, debug, and optimize apps on the Claude API and Anthropic SDK, including prompt caching and migrations.
entries.whyRecommended: Required if you're writing code against the Anthropic SDK. Encodes the right patterns instead of letting Claude guess.
